rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC EQUITIES, LLC VS. RUSSELL DANIEL COLE DBA DELTA T MECHANICAL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to properly allege any viable cause of action in good faith being mindful of all the exhibits attached to the original complaint, all allegations made so far, sham pleading doctrine and the statute of limitations. Each element of each cause of action must be supported by factual allegations, not conclusions. The court notes that Plaintiff failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B. Failure to comply in the future will result in motions being treated as unopposed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC. VS. MARK ANTHONY SMITH
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2022 line 4. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Second Amended Complaint was filed late without any explanation or leave of Court. In opposition Plaintiff appears to seek CCP 473(b) relief, but there is no motion on file and no evidence supporting the claim for surprise, mistake or excusable neglect (a passing note in the Points and Authorities regarding "mix-up" is not enough for CCP 473(b) relief or for the Court to exercise its discretion and deny the motion under CCP 581). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KATZ GROUP, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. KIM HALLMAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The operative complaint asserts two causes of action. While the demurrer purports to address the entire operative complaint (See Notice of Demurrer), only the demurrer to the second cause of action for forcible detainer is supported by Points and Authorities. Therefore, demurrer is overruled as to the entire first amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
